---
title: Node Classification
subtitle: Classifying and snapping WFRC network nodes to physical centerline endpoints
description: WFRC MasterNet node conflation — snapping abstract model nodes to UGRC/UDOT physical centerlines
author:
 - name: Pukar Bhandari
   email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov
   affiliation:
     - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council
       url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/"
date: "2026-04-03"
---

## Setup

### Imports

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import folium
import geopandas as gpd
import gtfs_kit as gk
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import shapely
from shapely.geometry import LineString
from shapely.strtree import STRtree

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd()))
from _src.node_utils import (
    nodes_on,
    count_links,
    count_neighbors,
    assign_node_directions,
    assign_endpoint_directions,
    snap_nodes,
    snap_transit,
)
```

### Configuration

```{python}
# ── Filepaths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATHS = {
    "in_centerlines": Path("_data/raw/RCL2TDMnew.gdb.zip"),
    "in_network_links": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet/WFv1000_MasterNet_20250821 - Link.shp"),
    "in_network_nodes": Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet/WFv1000_MasterNet_20250821 - Node.shp"),
    "in_gtfs": Path("_data/raw/uta_gtfs.zip"),
    "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"),
    "out_node_snapped": Path("_data/processed/nodes_snapped.gpkg"),
    "out_debug": Path("_data/processed/debug_snapping.gpkg"),
}

# ── CRS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CRS_UTM = "EPSG:26912"  # NAD83 UTM Zone 12N — Utah (metric)
CRS_WGS = "EPSG:4326"  # WGS84 — required by Folium
```

---

## Load Data

### Roadway Centerlines

```{python}
# 1. Prepare the Path
# We use .as_posix() to handle slashes correctly regardless of OS
# gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}"
gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines'].as_posix()}"

# 2. View the actual layers within the gdb
gpd.list_layers(gdb_path)
```

```{python}
COUNTY_FIPS = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
county_filter = "(" + ", ".join(f"'{f}'" for f in COUNTY_FIPS) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    gdb_path,  # f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}",
    layer="RCLWasatchFrontAll",
    where=f"(DOT_FCLASS IN ('Interstate', 'Local', 'Major Collector', 'Minor Arterial', 'Minor Collector', 'Other Freeway', 'Principal Arterial') OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%C%') AND CARTOCODE <> '99' AND (COUNTY_L IN {county_filter} OR COUNTY_R IN {county_filter})",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

# Strip trailing whitespace from categorical fields (source-data artefact)
for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Centerlines: {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Links and Nodes

```{python}
# Active 2027 links, excluding HOV / Managed Lane / Managed Lane Access (FT 37-39).
# These are filtered at load time so they never inflate LinkCount, contribute to
# the direction cascade, or generate spurious "surface" node classifications.
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(
    PATHS["in_network_links"],
    where="LN_2027 > 0 AND FT_2027 NOT IN (1, 37, 38, 39)",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Links: {len(gdf_links):,} (LN_2027 > 0, FT not in 1/37/38/39)")
print(f"Nodes: {len(gdf_nodes):,}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()
```

### GTFS Rail Stops

Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (TRAX, route_type 0) and Commuter Rail
(FrontRunner, route_type 2) only, walking the routes → trips → stop_times chain.

```{python}
feed = gk.read_feed(PATHS["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km")

gdf_stops  = gk.get_stops(feed,  as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = {0, 2}   # 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)

rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"]
rail_trip_ids  = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"]
rail_stop_ids  = feed.stop_times.loc[feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"].unique()

gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy()
print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
gdf_rail_stops.explore()
```

---

## Part A — Origin Processing (Network Nodes)

### A1 — Functional Type Flags

`nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, ft_codes)` returns True for any node whose
N appears as endpoint A or B of at least one link whose FT code is in `ft_codes`.

Flags are **non-exclusive** — a node touching both a freeway and a ramp link
will be True for both `Freeway` and `Ramp`. Mutual exclusion is enforced later
in the topology cascade (§A3).

```{python}
# ── Three mutually exclusive TDM link classes (2027 network year) ─────────
#
# FT_2027 code reference
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Class 1 — Freeway Mainline (FW_CODES):
#   12-15  Expressway (limited-access, divided)
#   22-26  MM freeway (posted 55-75 mph, MM series)
#   32-36  Freeway    (posted 55-75 mph, Fwy series)
#   40     Express / managed-access freeway
#   FT=2 AND DIRECTION=1: directional Principal Arterial links representing
#         physically separated roadways (e.g. divided expressways coded as PA).
#
# Class 2 — Ramps and CDs (RAMP_CODES):
#   20/30  Loop ramps  |  28/29  MM on/off ramps  |  41/42  Fwy on/off ramps
#   21/31  CD roads (collector-distributor geometry)
#
# Class 3 — Surface Streets (SURFACE_CODES):
#   2/3  Principal/Minor Arterial  |  4/5  Major/Minor Collector  |  6/7  Local/Unpaved
#   FT=2 links with DIRECTION=1 are excluded (they belong to Class 1 above).
#
# Filtered out at load time (not present in gdf_links):
#   1  Centroid connector (zone-to-network tap, not a physical road)
#   37 HOV lane  |  38 Managed lane  |  39 Managed-lane access point
#   Removed before any processing so they cannot inflate LinkCount/NeighborCount,
#   corrupt the pseudonode LinkClassCount check, pollute the direction cascade,
#   or produce spurious surface-type node classifications.
#
# Loaded but excluded from the three road classes:
#   70/80 Fixed transit  |  N ranges 10000-19999 and 50000-59999 (station nodes)
#   Transit geometry is handled separately in the FixedTransit snapping pass.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FT_COL = "FT_2027"

# DIRECTION=1 flag: handles both numeric 1 and string "1" encodings
_dir_is_1 = gdf_links["DIRECTION"].astype(str).str.strip() == "1"

# Mutually exclusive link masks (LN_2027 > 0 already filtered at load time)
FWY_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({12, 13, 14, 15, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40}) | (
    (gdf_links[FT_COL] == 2) & _dir_is_1
)
RAMP_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({20, 21, 28, 29, 30, 31, 41, 42})
SURFACE_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7}) & ~(
    (gdf_links[FT_COL] == 2) & _dir_is_1
)

# FW_CODES: FT codes used for the freeway-priority direction cascade.
# FT=2+DIRECTION=1 is classified as freeway but excluded here because its
# DIRECTION value is a one-way flag, not a cardinal (NB/SB/EB/WB).
# Direction matching for those links is deferred to the geometric heading
# approach planned for the ramp refactor (Request 3).
FW_CODES = {12, 13, 14, 15, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40}

gdf_nodes["Freeway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[FWY_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["Ramp"]    = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[RAMP_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["Surface"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[SURFACE_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = (
    nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, {70, 80})
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999)  # TRAX station nodes
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999)  # FrontRunner station nodes
)

flag_cols = ["Freeway", "Ramp", "Surface", "FixedTransit"]
print("Flag counts:")
print(gdf_nodes[flag_cols].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())
print(
    f"\nLinks by class — Freeway: {FWY_LINK_MASK.sum():,}  "
    f"| Ramp: {RAMP_LINK_MASK.sum():,}  "
    f"| Surface: {SURFACE_LINK_MASK.sum():,}"
)
```

### A2 — Link Count, Neighbor Count, and Direction

`count_links` adds `LinkCount` — raw endpoint references per node, used for the
distribution chart and diagnostics.

`count_neighbors` adds `NeighborCount` — the number of **distinct** neighboring
nodes. This is the topologically correct pseudonode test: `LinkCount` breaks on
bidirectional roads stored as two directional records (a pseudonode appears 4
times → LinkCount=4, not 2), while NeighborCount is always 2 for a true shape
vertex regardless of storage convention.

A node is an `is_pseudo` shape vertex if and only if both conditions hold:
1. **NeighborCount == 2** — lies on a single path between two real junctions.
2. **LinkClassCount == 1** — all connected links share the same functional class.
   A node where a freeway meets a surface street has NeighborCount=2 but
   LinkClassCount=2, so it is a real class-transition junction and must be snapped.

`assign_node_directions` produces two direction columns (see docstring):
- `link_directions` — freeway-priority cascade; used in passes (a) and (c).
- `fw_directions`   — freeway links only, majority-vote; used in pass (b) gore nodes.

```{python}
gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = count_neighbors(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = assign_node_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, freeway_ft_codes=FW_CODES)

# Assign one class label per link using the already-defined mutually exclusive masks.
_link_class = pd.Series("other", index=gdf_links.index)
_link_class[FWY_LINK_MASK]  = "freeway"
_link_class[RAMP_LINK_MASK] = "ramp"
_link_class[SURFACE_LINK_MASK] = "surface"
_link_class[gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({70, 80})] = "transit"

# For each node, count how many distinct link classes touch it.
_stacked_class = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
_class_counts = _stacked_class.groupby("N")["cls"].nunique()
gdf_nodes["LinkClassCount"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(_class_counts).fillna(0).astype(int)

# Pseudonode: shape vertex on a single homogeneous path — no physical snap target.
gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"] = (gdf_nodes["NeighborCount"] == 2) & (gdf_nodes["LinkClassCount"] == 1)

print(f"Pseudonodes (NeighborCount==2, single class): {gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'].sum():,}")
print(f"  of which LinkCount==2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] == 2)).sum():,}")
print(f"  of which LinkCount> 2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] > 2)).sum():,}  ← bidirectional road pseudonodes missed by old filter")
print(f"Class-transition nodes (NeighborCount==2, multi-class): {((gdf_nodes['NeighborCount'] == 2) & (gdf_nodes['LinkClassCount'] > 1)).sum():,}  ← real junctions, must be snapped")
```

```{python}
# Link-count distribution chart
link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

BASE_COLOR   = "#B4B2A9"
ACCENT_COLOR = "#1D9E75"
PSEUDO_COLOR = "#EF9F27"
LABEL_COLOR  = "#444441"
GRID_COLOR   = "#D3D1C7"

bar_colors = [
    PSEUDO_COLOR if lc == 2 else (ACCENT_COLOR if lc in {4, 6, 8} else BASE_COLOR)
    for lc in link_count_freq.index
]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")
ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0)
ax.set_axisbelow(True)

bars = ax.bar(link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values, color=bar_colors, width=0.75, zorder=2)
for bar in bars:
    h = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, h + max_val * 0.012,
            f"{int(h):,}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=9.5, color=LABEL_COLOR)

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)
ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))
ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors="#888780", length=0)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0)
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18)
ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", labelpad=10)

ax.legend(
    [plt.Rectangle((0,0),1,1,color=c) for c in [ACCENT_COLOR, PSEUDO_COLOR, BASE_COLOR]],
    ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"],
    loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False, labelcolor="#888780",
)
fig.text(0.13, 1.01, "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links",
         fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", color=LABEL_COLOR, transform=ax.transAxes)
fig.text(0.13, 0.94, f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes):,} network nodes by link count",
         fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", transform=ax.transAxes)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### A3 — Node Topology Classification

Eight mutually-exclusive `node_type` labels. Transit is evaluated first and
takes priority over all road-class combinations. The remaining seven labels
derive from the full combinatorics of the (Freeway, Ramp, Surface) flags.

HOV/managed-lane nodes (FT 37-39) were filtered at load time, so they
contribute no flags; those nodes fall to "surface" and are not snapped.
Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are shape vertices with no physical counterpart.

```{python}
no_pseudo = ~gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"]

is_fwy_node     = gdf_nodes["Freeway"]      & no_pseudo
is_ramp_node    = gdf_nodes["Ramp"]         & no_pseudo
is_surface_node = gdf_nodes["Surface"]      & no_pseudo
is_transit_node = gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] & no_pseudo

# Full combinatorics of the three link classes → 7 node types.
# Transit is evaluated first — station nodes get "transit" regardless of any
# road-class links they may also touch.
# Most-specific road combinations (all three flags) are evaluated next.
gdf_nodes["node_type"] = np.select(
    [
        is_transit_node,                                  # fixed rail station
        is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # fwy+ramp+sf → at-grade interchange
        is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # fwy+ramp    → elevated gore
        is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # fwy+sf      → at-grade fwy crossing
        is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # fwy only    → pure mainline
        ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # ramp+sf     → ramp terminal
        ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # ramp only   → mid-ramp
    ],
    ["transit", "gore_sf", "gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Node type distribution:")
print(gdf_nodes["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
```

```{python}
# Optional: inspect node flags and directions
gdf_nodes[["N", "LinkCount", "node_type", "link_directions", "fw_directions"]].head(20)
```

```{python}
out_path = PATHS["out_node_classified"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Classified nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```

---

## Part B — Target Processing (Centerline Endpoints)

The endpoint layer makes topology and direction assignments explicit and
inspectable in QGIS **before** any snapping happens. The full workflow is:
B1 filter to active segments → B2 classify segments → B3 extract endpoints →
B4 deduplicate → B5 assign directions → B6 assign ep_type → B7 export.

### B1 — Filter to Active Segments

```{python}
# Exclude retired / planned segments and segments that fall outside the three
# mutually exclusive classes (DupRoute routing artifacts, Institutional roads,
# and records with null/empty DOT_FCLASS that cannot be classified).
_status_ok  = ~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"])
_not_duprt  = gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("") != "DupRoute"
_fclass_ok  = (
    gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].notna()
    & ~gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Instituitional", ""])  # note: source-data typo
)
# Ramp/CD segments may have blank DOT_FCLASS but a valid DOT_RTNAME — keep them.
_is_ramp_seg = (
    gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("R", na=False)
    | gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("C", na=False)
) & _not_duprt

active_cl = gdf_centerlines[_status_ok & _not_duprt & (_fclass_ok | _is_ramp_seg)].copy()
print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}")
```

### B2 — Classify Segments

Three mutually exclusive boolean flags per segment. Primary authority is
`DOT_FCLASS` for freeway identification; `DOT_RTNAME` (UDOT LRS) supplements with
explicit route numbers and ramp/CD facility characters. Directional `NAME` tokens
(NB/SB/EB/WB) elevate divided Principal Arterials into the freeway class.

```
DOT_RTNAME layout (11 characters, zero-padded):
  [0:4]  Route number      e.g. "0015" = I-15, "0154" = Bangerter
  [4]    LRS direction     "P" = primary (NB/EB),  "N" = negative (SB/WB)
  [5]    Facility type     " " = mainline, "R" = ramp, "C" = collector-distributor
  [6:]   Sequence/suffix   varies
```

These flags flow into every endpoint pool and type-tier decision, making this
the **primary place to audit** when snapping results look wrong.

```{python}
# ── Controlled-access corridors in the Wasatch Front study area ───────────
# Adding / removing a route here is the primary tuning lever for freeway coverage.
# These are the 4-character UDOT route numbers (DOT_RTNAME[0:4]).
FREEWAY_ROUTES = {
    "0015",   # Interstate 15         (primary N-S spine)
    "0067",   # Legacy Parkway        (limited-access, no ramps)
    "0080",   # Interstate 80         (E-W, SLC to Parley's Canyon)
    "0084",   # Interstate 84         (Ogden corridor)
    "0089",   # US-89                 (surface in places — watch for mis-classification)
    "0092",   # Timpanogos Highway    (SR-92, American Fork Canyon)
    "0154",   # Bangerter Highway     (semi-controlled ring road)
    "0177",   # West Davis Corridor   (new build, limited segments)
    "0201",   # SR-201                (West Valley connector)
    "0215",   # Interstate 215        (belt route, SLC)
}

rtname  = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("")
# NAME column carries directional tokens (e.g. "I-215 NB") for physically
# separated roadways. Fall back to FULLNAME if NAME is not present.
_name_col = "NAME" if "NAME" in active_cl.columns else "FULLNAME"
name_str  = active_cl[_name_col].fillna("")

# ── Shared exclusion flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Ramp/CD character check: DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' or '%C%', excluding DupRoute.
_has_R_or_C = rtname.str.contains("R", na=False) | rtname.str.contains("C", na=False)
_not_duprt  = rtname != "DupRoute"
_not_ramp_cd = ~_has_R_or_C | ~_not_duprt  # True when segment is NOT a ramp/CD

# ── Class 1: Freeway Mainline ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Primary: DOT_FCLASS identifies interstate / other freeway geometry.
# Secondary: explicit UDOT route number is a controlled-access corridor.
# Tertiary: Principal Arterial with a directional name token (NB/SB/EB/WB)
#           represents a physically separated directional roadway.
# All three require exclusion of ramp/CD facility chars and DupRoute aliases.
_is_fclass_fwy = active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway"])
_is_named_fwy  = rtname.str.slice(0, 4).isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES)
_is_dir_pa = (active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"] == "Principal Arterial") & (
    name_str.str.contains("NB", na=False)
    | name_str.str.contains("SB", na=False)
    | name_str.str.contains("EB", na=False)
    | name_str.str.contains("WB", na=False)
)

active_cl["is_freeway"] = (
    (_is_fclass_fwy | _is_named_fwy | _is_dir_pa)
    & ~_has_R_or_C
    & _not_duprt
)

# ── Class 2: Ramps and CDs ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Strictly determined by DOT_RTNAME structure: any R or C facility character,
# excluding the DupRoute administrative alias.
active_cl["is_interchange"] = _has_R_or_C & _not_duprt

# ── Class 3: Surface Streets ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Everything that is not freeway mainline or ramp/CD.
# Explicitly excludes DOT_FCLASS values that fall outside the three classes
# (already filtered in B1, but guard here for completeness).
active_cl["is_surface"] = (
    ~active_cl["is_freeway"]
    & ~active_cl["is_interchange"]
    & active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].notna()
    & ~active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway", "Instituitional", ""])  # note: source-data typo
)

print("Segment-level classification:")
print(f"  is_freeway     (mainline):   {active_cl['is_freeway'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_interchange (ramp / CD):  {active_cl['is_interchange'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_surface     (all others): {active_cl['is_surface'].sum():,}")
print(f"  unclassified   (none):       {(~active_cl['is_freeway'] & ~active_cl['is_interchange'] & ~active_cl['is_surface']).sum():,}")
print(f"  both fwy + ic  (gore segs):  {(active_cl['is_freeway'] & active_cl['is_interchange']).sum():,}")
print(f"  total active:                {len(active_cl):,}")
```

### B3 — Extract Raw Endpoints

Extract the start and end point of every active segment into a flat GeoDataFrame
(two rows per segment). Carries `FULLNAME`, `DOT_RTNAME`, and the three topology
flags forward for direction resolution and deduplication downstream.

```{python}
# Explode MultiLineString → LineString so get_point works correctly
active_cl = active_cl.explode(index_parts=False).reset_index(drop=True)
print(
    f"After explode: {len(active_cl):,} segments (was {len(gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines['STATUS'].isin(['Retired', 'Planned'])]):,})"
)

starts = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, 0)
ends = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, -1)
print(f"Null starts after explode: {shapely.is_missing(starts).sum()}")  # should be 0

xs_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_x(starts), shapely.get_x(ends)])
ys_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_y(starts), shapely.get_y(ends)])
tile = lambda col: np.concatenate([active_cl[col].values, active_cl[col].values])

gdf_ep_raw = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "geometry": np.concatenate([starts, ends]),
        "seg_idx": tile("index")
        if "index" in active_cl.columns
        else np.concatenate([active_cl.index.values, active_cl.index.values]),
        "x_round": np.round(xs_all, 2),
        "y_round": np.round(ys_all, 2),
        "fullname": tile("FULLNAME"),
        "dot_rtname": tile("DOT_RTNAME"),
        "is_freeway": tile("is_freeway"),
        "is_interchange": tile("is_interchange"),
        "is_surface": tile("is_surface"),
        "is_start": np.concatenate([np.ones(len(active_cl), bool), np.zeros(len(active_cl), bool)]),
    },
    crs=CRS_UTM,
).reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"Raw endpoint records: {len(gdf_ep_raw):,}  (2 × {len(active_cl):,} segments)")
```

### B4 — Deduplicate by Coordinate

Group raw endpoints by rounded XY (millimetre precision). Aggregate type flags
with OR — a coordinate is `is_freeway=True` if any freeway segment terminates there.

```{python}
ep_agg = gdf_ep_raw.groupby(["x_round", "y_round"], as_index=False).agg(
    is_freeway=("is_freeway", "any"),
    is_interchange=("is_interchange", "any"),
    is_surface=("is_surface", "any"),
    fw_route_count=(
        "dot_rtname",
        lambda s: s[gdf_ep_raw.loc[s.index, "is_freeway"]].str[:4].nunique(),
    ),
)
ep_agg["geometry"] = gpd.points_from_xy(ep_agg["x_round"], ep_agg["y_round"])
gdf_ep_unique = gpd.GeoDataFrame(ep_agg, crs=CRS_UTM)

print(f"Unique endpoints: {len(gdf_ep_unique):,}")
print(
    f"  freeway-only   : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  freeway + ramp : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  ramp-only      : {(~gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_surface']).sum():,}"
)
print(f"  surface        : {gdf_ep_unique['is_surface'].sum():,}")
```

### B5 — Assign Endpoint Directions

`assign_endpoint_directions` calls `_resolve_direction` per unique coordinate,
aggregating direction from all contributing raw segments with freeway-first priority.

```{python}
gdf_ep_unique = assign_endpoint_directions(gdf_ep_unique, gdf_ep_raw)

print(f"Endpoints with direction: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] != '').sum():,}")
print(f"Endpoints direction-agnostic: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] == '').sum():,}")
```

### B6 — Assign Endpoint Type

Six mutually-exclusive `ep_type` labels derived from the three boolean flags,
mirroring the `node_type` cascade so type-tier matching compares like with like.
`gore` is checked first — a point where freeway and ramp both terminate is always
a gore regardless of surface contact.

```{python}
fw = gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"]
ic = gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]
sf = gdf_ep_unique["is_surface"]

gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] = np.select(
    [
        fw &  ic,            # freeway/ramp junction
        fw &  sf,            # at-grade freeway/surface crossing or frontage
        fw & ~ic & ~sf,      # pure freeway mainline endpoint
        ~fw & ic &  sf,      # ramp-to-arterial terminal
        ~fw & ic & ~sf,      # mid-ramp endpoint
    ],
    ["gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Endpoint type distribution:")
print(gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
```

```{python}
gdf_ep_unique[["x_round", "y_round", "is_freeway", "is_interchange", "is_surface",
               "ep_allowed_dirs", "ep_type"]].head(20)
```

### B7 — Export Endpoint Layer for QGIS Inspection

Verify before running any snapping:
- Freeway-only endpoints correctly identified (no ramp/CD bleeds)
- Gore endpoints at physical mainline/ramp junctions
- Direction strings match expected cardinal for each location

```{python}
debug_path = PATHS["out_debug"]
debug_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_ep_unique.to_file(debug_path, layer="endpoint_layer", driver="GPKG")
print(f"Endpoint layer → {debug_path} / layer='endpoint_layer'")
```

---

## Part C — Interchange Node Direction Enrichment

For interchange-only nodes (pass c), the directional signal is inherited one hop
from adjacent freeway mainline links. This enrichment is done **explicitly here**
(not hidden inside `snap_nodes`) so the lookup table is inspectable.

```{python}
# Build a flat edge list: (node_N, FT, DIRECTION) — vectorised via pd.concat
edges = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
edges["is_freeway_link"] = edges["FT_2027"].isin(FW_CODES)
edges["DIRECTION"]       = edges["DIRECTION"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

CARDINAL = {"NB", "SB", "EB", "WB"}

# For each interchange-only node, collect cardinals from adjacent freeway links.
ic_only_mask = ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node
ic_only_Ns   = set(gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "N"])

adj_fw = (
    edges[edges["N"].isin(ic_only_Ns) & edges["is_freeway_link"] & edges["DIRECTION"].isin(CARDINAL)]
    .groupby("N")["DIRECTION"]
    .apply(lambda s: ",".join(sorted(set(s))))
)

gdf_nodes["ic_fw_directions"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(adj_fw).fillna("")

resolved = (gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "ic_fw_directions"] != "").sum()
total_ic  = ic_only_mask.sum()
print(f"Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: {resolved:,} of {total_ic:,}")
print(f"  remaining {total_ic - resolved:,} are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link)")
```

---

## Part D — Node Snapping

Hierarchical execution — first-call-wins. Nodes snapped in an earlier pass are
skipped by subsequent passes.

### D1 — Freeway and Interchange Snapping

Four passes in topology-priority order: (a) pure freeway, (a2) at-grade freeway
crossings, (b) gore/gore_sf, (c) mid-ramp. All include `fwy_sf` in the D1 debug
export so at-grade crossing snaps can be verified before running surface snapping.

```{python}
# ── Endpoint pools ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Pass (a) — fwy nodes → fwy endpoints only.
#   Strict pool: no ramp or surface endpoints. Prevents divided-highway collapse
#   where NB and SB nodes both compete for the same non-directional endpoint.
ep_fwy_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"].copy()

# Pass (a2) — fwy_sf nodes → fwy_sf + fwy + gore endpoints.
#   fwy_sf nodes touch freeway links, so the freeway direction signal is reliable.
#   Surface endpoints are intentionally excluded from this pool — if no fwy-class
#   endpoint is within range the node falls through to D2 (surface pass) where
#   surface endpoints are available as a Tier-1 fallback.
ep_fwy_sf = (
    pd.concat(
        [
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy_sf"],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"],
        ]
    )
    .drop_duplicates()
    .copy()
)

# Pass (b) — gore + gore_sf nodes → gore endpoint pool.
#   Core pool: gore eps and multi-route fwy eps (major interchange hubs).
#   Extended with ramp_sf for gore_sf nodes (all three classes meet at grade).
#   _TYPE_TIER rejects ramp_sf for plain gore nodes, so the extension is harmless.
ep_gore = (
    pd.concat(
        [
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"],
            gdf_ep_unique[
                (gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy") & (gdf_ep_unique["fw_route_count"] >= 2)
            ],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "ramp_sf"],  # for gore_sf nodes
        ]
    )
    .drop_duplicates()
    .copy()
)

# Pass (c) — ramp nodes → ramp + ramp_sf endpoints.
#   ramp_sf nodes are excluded: they have surface links and route to D2 where
#   their at-grade topology is Tier-0.
ep_ic_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["ramp", "ramp_sf"])].copy()

print(f"Pass (a)  pool — fwy:                  {len(ep_fwy_only):,}")
print(f"Pass (a2) pool — fwy_sf + fwy + gore:  {len(ep_fwy_sf):,}")
print(f"Pass (b)  pool — gore + ramp_sf:       {len(ep_gore):,}")
print(f"Pass (c)  pool — ramp + ramp_sf:       {len(ep_ic_only):,}")

# ── Node masks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# gore_sf (fwy+ramp+surface): included in pass (b) alongside gore nodes;
#   _TYPE_TIER gives it access to ramp_sf endpoints at Tier-1.
# ramp_sf (ramp+surface): skips pass (c), routes directly to D2 surface pass.
print()
print(f"Pass (a)  nodes — fwy:     {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (a2) nodes — fwy_sf:  {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (b)  nodes — gore:    {(is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"               gore_sf:    {(is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (c)  nodes — ramp:    {(~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"  ramp_sf → D2 surface:   {(~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# ── Pre-snap distance check ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Confirm coverage and validate distance thresholds before committing.
fwy_ic_eligible = gdf_nodes[(is_fwy_node | is_ramp_node) & ~is_transit_node]
all_fwy_ep = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"] | gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]]

ep_geoms   = all_fwy_ep.geometry.to_numpy()
node_geoms = fwy_ic_eligible.geometry.to_numpy()

tree_pre     = STRtree(ep_geoms)
nearest_idxs = tree_pre.nearest(node_geoms)
pre_dists    = shapely.distance(node_geoms, ep_geoms[nearest_idxs])

print("Nearest endpoint distance — all freeway + interchange eligible nodes:")
print(f"  min:    {pre_dists.min():.1f}m")
print(f"  median: {np.median(pre_dists):.1f}m")
print(f"  p75:    {np.percentile(pre_dists, 75):.1f}m")
print(f"  p95:    {np.percentile(pre_dists, 95):.1f}m")
print(f"  max:    {pre_dists.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  ≤  50m: {(pre_dists <= 50).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists <= 50).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 150m: {(pre_dists <= 150).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists <= 150).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  > 300m: {(pre_dists > 300).sum():,}  ({(pre_dists > 300).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
```

```{python}
gdf_nodes_snapped = gdf_nodes.copy()

# _claimed: grows across passes — prevents two nodes in different passes from
# landing on the same physical endpoint.  The Gale-Shapley algorithm ensures
# uniqueness within a single pass; this set enforces it across passes whose
# endpoint pools overlap (e.g. gore endpoints appear in both ep_gore and
# ep_surface).
_claimed: frozenset = frozenset()

# Pass (a) — fwy nodes → fwy-only endpoint pool
#   Mask: fwy+ramp+surface combinations excluded — they go to passes (a2), (b), or D2.
#   Type tier: fwy ep = Tier-0 | gore/fwy_sf ep = Tier-1.
#   Direction: link_directions (freeway-priority cascade).
#   Threshold: 500m (model centroids can sit well off the physical carriageway).
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_fwy_only,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Only",
    direction_col="link_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (a2) — fwy_sf nodes (fwy+surface, no ramp) → fwy_sf endpoint pool
#   At-grade freeway crossings: the node touches a freeway AND a surface street.
#   Type tier: fwy_sf ep = Tier-0 | fwy/gore ep = Tier-1.
#   Surface endpoints are NOT in this pool — nodes that miss here fall to D2 where
#   surface endpoints are available as a graceful fallback.
#   Direction: link_directions (freeway component provides the cardinal signal).
#   Threshold: 500m.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_fwy_sf,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Surface",
    direction_col="link_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (b) — gore + gore_sf nodes → gore endpoint pool
#   gore:    fwy+ramp, no surface — classic elevated junction.
#            Type tier: gore ep = Tier-0 | fwy/ramp/fwy_sf ep = Tier-1.
#   gore_sf: fwy+ramp+surface — at-grade interchange.
#            Type tier: gore ep = Tier-0 | fwy_sf/ramp_sf/fwy/ramp ep = Tier-1.
#            ramp_sf eps are in the pool for gore_sf; _TYPE_TIER rejects them
#            for plain gore nodes, so no false matches on elevated junctions.
#   Direction: fw_directions (freeway-only majority-vote, isolates mainline
#              direction from the ramp/CD signal at the same node).
#   Threshold: 500m.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_gore,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Gore",
    direction_col="fw_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (c) — ramp nodes (mid-ramp only, no surface) → ramp endpoint pool
#   Direction: ic_fw_directions (inherited one hop from adjacent freeway — see Part C).
#   ramp nodes have no surface links so link_directions is unreliable;
#   freeway-neighbor direction is the best available signal.
#   Threshold: 100m (mid-ramp nodes sit close to physical endpoints).
#   fwy_sf and ramp_sf nodes are intentionally excluded — both have surface links
#   and route to the surface pass (D2) where their at-grade topology is Tier-0.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_ic_only,
    node_mask=~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=100, label="Interchange_Only",
    direction_col="ic_fw_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)
```

### D1 Summary

```{python}
FWY_IC_RULES = ["Freeway_Only", "Freeway_Surface", "Freeway_Gore", "Interchange_Only"]

for rule in FWY_IC_RULES:
    dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == rule, "snap_distance_m"]
    if len(dists) == 0:
        print(f"{rule}: 0 nodes snapped")
        continue
    print(f"{rule} ({len(dists):,} nodes):")
    print(f"  median {dists.median():.1f}m  |  p90 {dists.quantile(0.9):.1f}m  |  max {dists.max():.1f}m")
    print(f"  < 50m: {(dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")

exceeded_fwy_ic = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    (is_fwy_node | is_ramp_node) & ~is_transit_node & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
]
print(f"\nNodes exceeding threshold (all D1 passes): {len(exceeded_fwy_ic):,}")
if len(exceeded_fwy_ic) > 0:
    print(exceeded_fwy_ic["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())
    print(f"\nExpected recycling to D2 surface pass:")
    print(f"  ramp_sf:  {(exceeded_fwy_ic['node_type'] == 'ramp_sf').sum():,}  (ramp terminals — surface ep is Tier-0)")
    print(f"  fwy_sf:   {(exceeded_fwy_ic['node_type'] == 'fwy_sf').sum():,}   (fwy crossings that failed pass (a2) — no fwy ep within range)")
    print(f"  gore_sf:  {(exceeded_fwy_ic['node_type'] == 'gore_sf').sum():,}  (at-grade interchanges that failed pass (b))")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

# Folium validation map — Freeway + Interchange snapping
fwy_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(FWY_IC_RULES)]
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index]

fwy_snapped_wgs = fwy_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
fwy_original_wgs = fwy_original.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors = [
    {
        "geometry": LineString(
            [fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx], fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]]
        ),
        "N": int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"]),
        "dist_m": float(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
    }
    for idx in fwy_snapped.index
    if fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx].distance(fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]) > 0
]
gdf_fwy_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors, crs=CRS_UTM)

RULE_COLORS = {
    "Freeway_Only": "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Surface": "#17A5C5",
    "Freeway_Gore": "#534AB7",
    "Interchange_Only": "#EF9F27",
}

center = [fwy_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), fwy_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

conn_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Displacement lines", show=True)
for _, row in gdf_fwy_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780",
        weight=1,
        opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(conn_layer)
conn_layer.add_to(m)

orig_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Original positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=4,
        color="#E24B4A",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(orig_layer)
orig_layer.add_to(m)

snap_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Snapped positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    rule = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"]
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=4,
        color=RULE_COLORS.get(rule, "#888780"),
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {rule} | {fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(snap_layer)
snap_layer.add_to(m)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m)
m
```

### D2 — Surface Snapping

All nodes with surface-class links that were not snapped in earlier passes land
here. This includes: pure `surface` nodes, `ramp_sf` (ramp terminals), any
`fwy_sf` that exceeded the pass (a2) threshold (no fwy-class endpoint within
range), and any `gore_sf` that failed pass (b). `link_directions` carries the
cardinal signal from all connected links.

```{python}
# Surface pool: surface, ramp_sf, fwy_sf, and gore endpoints.
# gore included for ramp_sf / gore_sf nodes that sit physically at a fwy/ramp junction.
# _TYPE_TIER keeps surface nodes from matching gore eps (hard-reject), so adding
# gore to the pool does not cause surface nodes to snap to interchange endpoints.
ep_surface = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["surface", "ramp_sf", "fwy_sf", "gore"])].copy()
print(f"Surface endpoint pool: {len(ep_surface):,}")
print(f"  surface-only: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'surface').sum():,}")
print(f"  ramp+surface: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'ramp_sf').sum():,}")
print(f"  fwy+surface:  {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'fwy_sf').sum():,}")
print(f"  gore:         {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_type'] == 'gore').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# surface_mask: any node with Surface=True that wasn't snapped in an earlier pass.
# Covers: surface, ramp_sf, fwy_sf, and gore_sf nodes (all have Surface flag = True).
# Pseudonodes excluded via is_pseudo (NeighborCount==2 + single link class).
# Transit nodes excluded — rail stations snap only to GTFS stops in D3.
# First-call-wins ensures gore_sf nodes already snapped in pass (b) are skipped.
surface_mask = (
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["Surface"])
    & ~gdf_nodes_snapped["is_pseudo"]
    & (gdf_nodes_snapped["node_type"] != "transit")
)
_unsnapped = gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(["none", "exceeded_threshold"])
print(f"Surface nodes eligible (unsnapped): {(surface_mask & _unsnapped).sum():,}")
print(f"  ramp_sf:          {(surface_mask & _unsnapped & (gdf_nodes_snapped['node_type'] == 'ramp_sf')).sum():,}")
print(f"  fwy_sf (fallback):{(surface_mask & _unsnapped & (gdf_nodes_snapped['node_type'] == 'fwy_sf')).sum():,}")
print(f"  gore_sf (fallback):{(surface_mask & _unsnapped & (gdf_nodes_snapped['node_type'] == 'gore_sf')).sum():,}")
print(f"  surface:          {(surface_mask & _unsnapped & (gdf_nodes_snapped['node_type'] == 'surface')).sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_surface,
    node_mask      = surface_mask,
    max_distance_m = 400,   # wider than pass (c) — ramp_sf terminals can be displaced
    label          = "Surface",
    direction_col  = "link_directions",
    target_id_cols = ["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords = _claimed,
)
```

```{python}
surf_dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface", "snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Surface nodes snapped:  {len(surf_dists):,}")
print(f"  median: {surf_dists.median():.1f}m  |  max: {surf_dists.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  < 50m:   {(surf_dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(surf_dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  50-200m: {((surf_dists >= 50) & (surf_dists <= 200)).sum():,}")
print(f"  200-400m:{((surf_dists > 200) & (surf_dists <= 400)).sum():,}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

# Folium validation map — Surface snapping
surf_snapped_gdf = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]
surf_original_gdf = gdf_nodes.loc[surf_snapped_gdf.index]
surf_snapped_wgs = surf_snapped_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
surf_original_wgs = surf_original_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors_surf = [
    {
        "geometry": LineString(
            [surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx], surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]]
        ),
        "N": int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "N"]),
        "dist_m": float(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
    }
    for idx in surf_snapped_gdf.index
    if surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx].distance(surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]) > 0
]
gdf_surf_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors_surf, crs=CRS_UTM)

center = [surf_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), surf_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_surf = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")
for _, row in gdf_surf_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780",
        weight=1,
        opacity=0.5,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_surf)
for idx, row in surf_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=3,
        color="#E24B4A",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(m_surf)
for idx, row in surf_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=3,
        color="#1D9E75",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_surf)
folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_surf)
m_surf
```

### D3 — Transit Snapping

FixedTransit nodes snap to Light Rail (TRAX) and Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)
stops. Bus stops are excluded. Twin platforms merged to station centroid.

```{python}
# Merge co-located platforms (same stop_name) to a single centroid.
# geometry column is plain shapely objects after the lambda — no inherited CRS.
# gdf_rail_stops is already in CRS_UTM (reprojected at import), so set directly.
rail_stops_merged = (
    gdf_rail_stops.groupby("stop_name")
    .apply(
        lambda g: pd.Series(
            {
                "stop_id": ",".join(g["stop_id"].astype(str).unique()),
                "geometry": g.geometry.union_all().centroid,
            }
        ),
        include_groups=False,
    )
    .reset_index()
)
gdf_rail_stops_grouped = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    rail_stops_merged,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs=CRS_UTM,
)

print(f"Grouped rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops_grouped):,}")
```

```{python}
transit_mask = gdf_nodes_snapped["node_type"] == "transit"
print(f"Transit nodes eligible: {transit_mask.sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_transit(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    gdf_rail_stops_grouped,
    node_mask=transit_mask,
    max_distance_m=200,
    label="FixedTransit_Rail",
)
```

```{python}
transit_dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "FixedTransit_Rail", "snap_distance_m"
]
print(f"Transit nodes snapped: {len(transit_dists):,}")
print(f"  median: {transit_dists.median():.1f}m  |  max: {transit_dists.max():.1f}m")
```

---

## Debug Export

Unified debug package covering all snapping passes. Every layer carries `snap_rule`
and `node_type` so QGIS can filter by pass (e.g. show only `Freeway_Only`) or by
topology class (e.g. show only `ramp_sf` ramp terminals) — or display everything at
once. The `endpoint_layer` was already written in step B7.

`node_type` to topology mapping for QGIS filters:

| `node_type` | Topology |
|---|---|
| `transit` | Fixed rail station (TRAX / FrontRunner) |
| `fwy` | Freeway mainline only |
| `gore` | Freeway + ramp (elevated) |
| `gore_sf` | Freeway + ramp + surface (at-grade) |
| `fwy_sf` | Freeway + surface (at-grade crossing) |
| `ramp` | Ramp only (mid-ramp) |
| `ramp_sf` | Ramp + surface (ramp terminal) |
| `surface` | Surface street only |

```{python}
# All successfully snapped nodes across every pass
all_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"]].copy()

# Original positions with snap metadata attached for QGIS filtering
all_original = gdf_nodes.loc[all_snapped.index].copy()
for col in ["snap_rule", "node_type", "snap_distance_m"]:
    all_original[col] = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[all_snapped.index, col]

# Displacement lines — one per snapped node that actually moved
disp_lines = [
    {
        "N": int(all_snapped.loc[idx, "N"]),
        "snap_rule": all_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"],
        "node_type": all_snapped.loc[idx, "node_type"],
        "snap_distance_m": float(all_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
        "geometry": LineString(
            [all_original.loc[idx, "geometry"], all_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]]
        ),
    }
    for idx in all_snapped.index
    if all_original.loc[idx, "geometry"].distance(all_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]) > 0
]
gdf_disp_lines = (
    gpd.GeoDataFrame(disp_lines, crs=CRS_UTM)
    if disp_lines
    else gpd.GeoDataFrame(
        columns=["N", "snap_rule", "node_type", "snap_distance_m", "geometry"], crs=CRS_UTM
    )
)

# All nodes that exceeded their pass threshold — includes node_type for triage
all_unsnapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold"].copy()

all_snapped.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(debug_path, layer="snapped_positions", driver="GPKG")
all_original.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(debug_path, layer="original_positions", driver="GPKG")
if not gdf_disp_lines.empty:
    gdf_disp_lines.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(debug_path, layer="displacement_lines", driver="GPKG")
if not all_unsnapped.empty:
    all_unsnapped.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(debug_path, layer="unsnapped_nodes", driver="GPKG")

print(f"Debug package → {debug_path}")
print(f"  endpoint_layer:     (written in B7)")
print(f"  snapped_positions:  {len(all_snapped):,}")
snap_rule_counts = all_snapped["snap_rule"].value_counts()
for rule, count in snap_rule_counts.items():
    print(f"    {rule}: {count:,}")
print(f"  displacement_lines: {len(gdf_disp_lines):,}")
print(f"  unsnapped_nodes:    {len(all_unsnapped):,}")
```

---

## Overall Snap Summary

```{python}
print("Results by snap rule:")
print(gdf_nodes_snapped.value_counts("snap_rule").rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())
print(f"\nTotal:              {len(gdf_nodes_snapped):,}")
print(f"Snapped:            {gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].sum():,}"
      f"  ({gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Exceeded threshold: {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'exceeded_threshold').sum():,}")
print(f"Not attempted:      {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'none').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# Exceeded-threshold detail table
bool_cols = [c for c in gdf_nodes_snapped.columns if gdf_nodes_snapped[c].dtype == bool]
gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold"][
    ["N", "snap_distance_m"] + bool_cols
].sort_values("snap_distance_m", ascending=False)
```

### Conflation Performance — KPIs and ECDF

```{python}
snapped_nodes = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"]]

kpi_table = (
    snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule")["snap_distance_m"]
    .agg(
        Nodes_Snapped="count",
        Median_Dist_m="median",
        P90_Dist_m=lambda x: np.percentile(x, 90),
        Max_Dist_m="max",
    )
    .round(1)
)
print("=== SNAPPING PERFORMANCE KPIs ===")
print(kpi_table.to_string())
print("\nNodes exceeding threshold:", (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold").sum())
```

```{python}
COLOR_MAP = {
    "Freeway_Only":      "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Surface":   "#17A5C5",
    "Freeway_Gore":      "#534AB7",
    "Interchange_Only":  "#EF9F27",
    "Surface":           "#378ADD",
    "FixedTransit_Rail": "#888780",
}

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

for rule, group_data in snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule"):
    distances = np.sort(group_data["snap_distance_m"].dropna())
    y = np.arange(1, len(distances) + 1) / len(distances)
    ax.step(distances, y, where="post",
            label=f"{rule} (n={len(distances):,})",
            color=COLOR_MAP.get(rule, "#000000"), linewidth=2)

ax.set_xlim(0, 300)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.6)
ax.set_title("Algorithm Precision: Cumulative Snapping Distances", fontsize=14, pad=15)
ax.set_xlabel("Displacement Distance (meters)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Percentage of Nodes Snapped", fontsize=11)
ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=10, frameon=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export

```{python}
out_path = PATHS["out_node_snapped"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes_snapped.to_crs(CRS_UTM).to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Snapped nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```
